articles.title: Rs 10 LPA to Rs 20 LPA, but still broke? CA warns about a lifestyle creep quietly draining your money
articles.description: Professionals often feel financially stuck despite salary increases because expenses rise just as quickly as income. This phenomenon, known as lifestyle creep, sees new earnings absorbed by upgrades like better housing and premium services, preventing wealth accumulation. Experts advise prioritizing investments over immediate lifestyle enhancements to build long-term financial security.

articles.title: Powerball jackpot surges to $113 million for May 20 drawing - Here's what the winner could actually pocket on Wednesday night
articles.description: The Powerball jackpot has climbed to an estimated $113 million for the May 20 drawing after no one matched the winning numbers on May 18. Players across the US are hoping for a life-changing win, with options for a lump sum or annuity payout. The next drawing is set for Wednesday. Despite the extremely low odds of around 1 in 292 million, millions continue to take part in the draw, drawn by the dream of instant wealth.

articles.title: Did Trump's IRS settlement permanently shield his past tax matters? New clause sparks outrage- here's what the new clause is
articles.description: A new settlement term bars the IRS from investigating President Trump, his family, and businesses for past tax issues. This extraordinary addition to a $10 billion lawsuit resolution permanently prevents claims or examinations arising from tax returns filed before the agreement. Critics have labeled the move self-dealing and corruption.

articles.title: Daily numerology horoscope, May 20, 2026: Financial predictions and money guidance for numbers 1 to 9
articles.description: Daily Numerology 20th May 2026 predicts financial growth, careful planning, investment opportunities, and long-term stability for all birth numbers.

articles.title: Fuel price hike hits Lucknowites wallets, commuters feel the pinch
articles.description: At petrol pumps across Lucknow, residents said that while incomes have remained largely unchanged, household expenses for food, education, healthcare and transport have increased steadily over the past few months. Residents noted that the impact of rising fuel prices extends beyond transportation, as higher logistics costs eventually push up prices of vegetables, groceries and other essential commodities.

articles.title: From ORS sachets to sun caps: Delhi government to distribute summer kits to construction workers to tackle heat
articles.description: As part of the heatwave action plan, officials said that the government is planning to distribute summer kits to labourers and workers at construction sites during peak summer months.

articles.title: Ludhiana: Transport Nagar residents raise concerns over garbage dumping, poor infrastructure
articles.description: Sonu, who works in Transport Nagar, said waste is regularly dumped in the area while sewerage lines remain blocked for long periods

articles.title: Ludhiana to begin ration distribution, aims to avoid technical glitches
articles.description: Ludhiana has around 4.40 lakh ration card holders and nearly 22,000 metric tonnes of wheat is expected to be distributed till June 30, say officials

articles.title: Rupee tumbles to record low of 96.86 vs USD as US-Iran stalemate stokes global inflation fears
articles.description: The rupee hit a record low against the dollar on Wednesday, driven by elevated oil prices stemming from stalled U.S.-Iran peace talks. This surge in energy costs fuels inflation fears, pushing bond yields higher and impacting equities. The rupee's decline, coupled with significant overseas investor withdrawals and rising Brent crude prices, exacerbates India's balance of payments deficit.

articles.title: Delhi Confidential: Taking Stock
Top fertiliser companies float tender to buy 600k t Sulphur
articles.description: Top Indian fertiliser firms have united to buy a large quantity of sulphur. This critical raw material is needed for phosphatic fertilisers. The collective move signals preparedness for ongoing global supply disruptions and rising prices. India relies heavily on imports for key fertilisers and their components.

articles.title: Stocks in news: Lenskart, Apollo Hospitals, ZEE, BPCL, Hindalco
articles.description: Indian markets navigated a sideways trend, with analysts urging caution until the 24,000 mark is reclaimed. Key stocks like Lenskart, Apollo Hospitals, ZEE, BPCL, and Hindalco are in focus due to Q4 results and news. BEL reported a profit rise, while ZEE saw a net loss. Maruti Suzuki expanded production capacity.

articles.title: Delhi-NCR to get highway projects worth Rs 34,000 cr: Gadkari
articles.description: Delhi-NCR is set to receive a major transport upgrade. Over ₹34,000 crore will be invested in new highway projects. These include expressways, ring roads, tunnels, and elevated corridors. The government aims to create a modern and future-ready transport network. This initiative will boost connectivity, reduce fuel use, and promote economic growth in the region.

articles.title: Cement cos diverge on expansion plans amid West Asia uncertainty
articles.description: Indian cement companies are taking different paths on spending. Some, like Ultratech Cement, Dalmia Bharat, and Nuvoco Vistas, are increasing investments for expansion. Others, including Ambuja Cement and Shree Cement, are slowing down their capital expenditure. This divergence reflects varied growth strategies in the Indian market. The focus remains on India's long-term development drivers.

articles.title: Nordic investment in India up 200% in last decade, says PM Modi
articles.description: PM Modi, at the India-Nordic Summit, said Nordic investments in India rose by 200% in a decade. He noted that deepening financial integration has fostered commercial ties, boosted mutual growth, and created substantial employment opportunities.

articles.title: Why are oil prices down today and will Brent, US WTI crude futures continue to drop or rise again? Oil settles lower after Iran talks progress
articles.description: Why are oil prices down today and will Brent, US WTI crude futures continue to drop or rise again? Oil prices fell after the United States said talks with Iran showed progress and military action was paused. Brent and WTI futures dropped after recent highs. Supply risks in the Middle East, Chinese refinery cuts, sanctions, SPR drawdowns, and refinery disruptions continue to shape market direction and investor outlook for crude prices.

articles.title: Bengaluru: All manholes to be desilted by May-end, says BWSSB
articles.description: BWSSB aims to finish desilting Bengaluru's overflowing manholes by the end of May to mitigate monsoon issues.

articles.title: Insurers resist as customer comfort weighs on exec pay
articles.description: Indian insurers are opposing new rules for executive pay. The regulator wants to link compensation to customer service, not just profits. Insurers argue a one-size-fits-all approach won't work for all companies. They seek flexibility in setting performance goals. This comes amid increased scrutiny of claims and customer service.

articles.title: Ind-Ra says West Asia tensions, El Nino to drag FY27 growth to 6.7%
articles.description: India's economic growth is projected to slow to 6.7% in 2026-27. Higher crude oil and food prices, along with West Asia conflict uncertainty, will impact growth. A potential El Nino event could also affect agriculture. India Ratings and Research (Ind-Ra) made this projection. Inflation is expected to be 4.4% in 2026-27.

articles.title: Gold Silver Rate Today (May 19): Prices Rise Across Major Cities; Check Latest Rates In Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, More
articles.description:
Gold Silver Rate Today: Gold and silver prices remained volatile on Tuesday as investors reacted to rising geopolitical tensions in West Asia, elevated crude oil prices and a sharp jump in US Treasury yields.

On the Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX), gold futures for June 5 delivery were trading at Rs 1,60,001 per 10 grams around 6 pm, up Rs 600 or 0.38 per cent. During the session, the yellow metal swung sharply between gains and losses, touching an intraday high of Rs 1,59,772 and a low of Rs 1,59,401.

Silver prices also witnessed sharp fluctuations. MCX silver futures for July 3 delivery were trading at Rs 2,74,950 per kg, down Rs 1,701 or 0.61 per cent. Earlier in the session, silver had fallen as much as Rs 2,74,519 per kg before rebounding to an intraday high of Rs 2,76,651.

Analysts said bullion prices remained under pressure globally as stronger US inflation data pushed Treasury yields and the US dollar higher, reducing expectations of near-term US Federal Reserve rate cuts.

Market experts added that concerns over energy supply disruptions in West Asia and rising crude oil prices have intensified inflation worries, increasing expectations of tighter monetary policy globally and weighing on non-yielding assets such as gold and silver.

Silver sentiment was also impacted after UBS cut its full-year silver investment demand forecast and projected a narrower global supply deficit.

In global markets, COMEX gold slipped 0.37 per cent to $4,541 per ounce, while COMEX silver declined 1.24 per cent to $76.48 per ounce.

Meanwhile, crude oil prices fell after reports of an attack on a nuclear facility in the United Arab Emirates raised fears of supply disruptions. Brent crude fell 1.65 per cent to $110.32 per barrel, while WTI crude fell 1.07 per cent to $103.26 per barrel.
